items.title: Milky Mist set to launch India's biggest dairy IPO in early August
items.summary: Milky Mist Dairy Food plans its initial public offering for early August.  This upcoming IPO is anticipated to be the largest by an Indian dairy company.  The company secured ₹482 crore in pre-IPO funding from Jongsong Investments.  This investment valued Milky Mist at approximately ₹9,300 crore based on share price.  The market debut follows a significant pre-IPO funding round this May.

items.title: Dollar rides yield surge as Middle East and trade wars raise inflation stakes
items.summary: The dollar strengthened against major currencies on Friday.  Rising oil prices and new trade tariffs fueled inflation concerns globally.  U.S. Treasury yields climbed significantly, reaching multi-month highs.  The yen remained near a 40-year low against the dollar.  Investors anticipate central bank decisions amid inflationary pressures.

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | When will Indian stocks look up again? An end to this year’s oil shock will help
items.summary: India Inc’s last-quarter earnings hold promise while economic conditions show signs of improvement, but the war in West Asia has cast a shadow on market sentiment again. Investors need relief from energy-cost uncertainty.

items.title: NSE cash market turnover hits 23-month high in June
items.summary: Indian equity markets experienced robust trading in June, reaching a 23-month high in cash market turnover. Proprietary traders' share of cash market turnover decreased, indicating broader investor participation. Foreign investors significantly increased their presence in equity futures, reaching a record high. Domestic institutional investors also boosted their share in equity futures during this period. Individual investors raised their participation in equity options, showing growing retail trader involvement.

items.title: Titan's bullish momentum strengthens at record highs; Shivangi Sarda recommends Buy
Financials dominate Nifty50 in June, IT sector weight hits 23-year low
items.summary: Financials increased their Nifty 50 weight to a four-month high in June. This rise followed sector outperformance and renewed foreign investor inflows. Information Technology continued its decline, losing significant weight in the index. The IT sector's weight is near a 23-year low due to global spending concerns. Weak global technology spending and AI disruption also impacted the IT sector.

items.title: Smaller stocks gain ground as largecaps lose market share
items.summary: Large-cap stocks have seen their market share decline over six years. Mid, small, and micro-cap stocks have gained significant ground during this period. The Nifty's share of total market capitalization fell to a multi-year low. Meanwhile, the Nifty Smallcap 250 reached a multi-year high. This trend highlights the increasing weight of smaller companies in the market.

items.title: D-St set for a negative opening as GIFT Nifty signals weak start
items.summary: Domestic equities extended losses for a fourth consecutive session, with the Nifty declining. Investor sentiment remained fragile amid a sharp surge in Brent crude oil prices. Foreign portfolio investors net sold shares worth Rs 2,999 crore on Wednesday. The rupee traded in a narrow range, appreciating marginally by around 0.05%. Analysts suggest Indian equities are likely to remain under pressure in the near term.

items.title: Stocks in news: Infosys, Cipla, Meesho, Suzlon Energy, IndiGo, NTPC
items.summary: Markets extended their losing streak for a fourth consecutive session. Infosys reported a 12% year-on-year profit growth in its first quarter. Cipla's net profit fell 39% due to weakness in its North America business. IndiGo reported a loss of Rs 238 crore in its first quarter. Several other companies will announce their first quarter results today.

items.title: Infosys ADR slips over 5% after Q1 results, FY27 growth guidance cut
items.summary: Infosys ADR fell after the company reported a sequential drop in net profit for the first quarter of FY27 and a cut in the upper end of its FY27 revenue growth guidance.

items.title: Global Market Today: Asian stocks fall on tech selloff, oil trades above $100
items.summary: Asian stocks declined following a Wall Street tech selloff and investor doubts about artificial intelligence returns. Oil prices held their gains after closing over one hundred dollars, reaching a May high.  Treasuries fell and the dollar strengthened as inflation and interest rate hike concerns grew.  US President Donald Trump's threats intensified conflict worries after Houthi militants attacked oil tankers.  These events fueled concerns over potential disruptions to global energy supplies.

items.title: Nuclear fusion startups seek regulatory clarity, more patient capital
items.summary: One of the key recommendations from fusion startups is to create a separate regulatory framework for fusion reactors, arguing that the current law largely applies rules designed for conventional fission reactors

items.title: Edge data centres expand beyond metros despite early hurdles
items.summary: Attracted by capital subsidies, cheaper land and tax incentives, operators are setting up facilities in cities such as Bhubaneswar, Patna, Jaipur and Kolkata.

items.title: Stocks to watch: NTPC, Shriram Finance, Infosys among shares in focus today; check list here
items.summary: Stock market today: On Thursday, Sensex shed 364 points, or 0.47%, to close at 76,391.39, while the Nifty 50 fell 127 points, or 0.53%, to settle at 23,869.60.

items.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today — 24 July 2026
items.summary: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five breakout stocks to buy today — Kabra Extrusion, Ask Automotive, Pricol, Nuvama Wealth Management, and Manali Petrochemicals

items.title: Caliber Mining and Logistics IPO listing date today. GMP, experts signal debut at decent premium in Indian stock market
items.summary: Caliber Mining and Logistics IPO listing date is 24 July 2026, Friday. Ahead of the share debt, Caliber Mining IPO GMP and analysts signal a strong listing of shares on Dalal Street.

items.title: Sebi introduces fast-track process for securities transmission
items.summary: Sebi has revised the securities transmission framework for investors.  A fast-track mechanism is introduced for small-value claims.  Documentation requirements are simplified, and probate of a will is removed.  Claims up to ₹10 lakh in physical and ₹30 lakh in demat form are covered.  This aims for a more efficient and investor-friendly process.

items.title: Private bank ETFs back in focus as valuations turn attractive
items.summary: Private bank stocks present a thematic investment opportunity with reasonable valuations.  These banks are showing improved margins and strong credit growth potential.  Analysts believe margin pressures are now behind the banking sector.  Credit growth is expected to accelerate significantly over the next two years.  Investors can consider accumulating private banks with a medium-term outlook.

items.title: US imposes 10% tariff on India, Pak, Bangladesh, UK, others in forced labour probe
items.summary: The United States has imposed 10% tariffs on goods from India and sixteen other nations. This action stems from their alleged failure to ban imports made with forced labor. India amended its foreign trade policy to prohibit such imports after the investigation began. Trading partners adopting and enforcing these prohibitions will face a 10% tariff rate. This move aims to address human rights abuses and distortive trade practices globally.

items.title: Consulting partner pool soars, taps academia, policy and tech too
items.summary: Consulting and Big Four firms in India have more than doubled their partner ranks over last three to five years amid rising demand for specialized expertise. They are also hiring beyond traditional consulting into tech, academia, and startups as AI reshapes client needs.

items.title: Can Lohia Corp IPO deliver long-term growth for high risk investors?
items.summary: Lohia Corp plans an offer for sale to raise funds.  The company's revenue grew significantly while its net profit surged.  Its order book expanded substantially after strong financial performance.  The business is cyclical, depending on agriculture and construction sectors.  Investors with higher risk appetite may consider this long-term opportunity.

items.title: Income Tax Day 2026: 5 myths about income tax that still confuse taxpayers
items.summary: Income Tax Day is celebrated on July 24 every year. The day honors Sir James Wilson, the first Finance Member of British India, who instituted income tax in India in 1860.

items.title: Stock recommendations for 24 July from MarketSmith India
items.summary: MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 24 July. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions.

items.title: Irdai plans to roll out risk-based capital norms by April. Are insurers ready?
items.summary: This will help insurers value their assets, liabilities and investments better, more accurately provide for their risk exposure, and increase transparency and disclosures for all stakeholders.

items.title: Raja Venkatraman recommends three stocks for 24 July
items.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 24 July. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.

items.title: The Street resets its Nifty 500 bets. Here are the winners
items.summary: The optimism reflects the underperformance of Indian equities relative to other emerging markets and the US, along with a moderation in valuations.

items.title: Temasek-backed Manipal Health to line up $1-billion IPO
items.summary: Manipal Health Enterprises plans a billion-dollar initial public offering this month.  The company will announce its price band on July twenty-fourth.  The offering includes a fresh issue of shares and an offer for sale.  Proceeds will primarily be used to reduce debt and acquire a minority stake.  This follows SBI Funds Management's successful large IPO earlier this year.

items.title: North India leads India's expanding stock market investor base
items.summary: North India now leads the nation in registered investors, surpassing the West. This significant shift accelerated after the pandemic with increased internet access. The North region saw its investor base expand six times since FY19. Maharashtra remains the largest state, though its share has declined. Uttar Pradesh has now overtaken Gujarat as the second-largest investor state.

items.title: Sebi plans a wider investment play for portfolio managers
items.summary: The regulator said the review has been done due the rapid expansion of the PMS industry, where AUM (assets under management) has multiplied to ₹42.61 lakh crore as of May 2026 from ₹18.07 lakh crore in April 2019. "Considering the increasing sophistication of investors, growing demand for more personalised solutions and diverse investment portfolio, a need was felt to review the PM (portfolio manager) Regulations," Sebi said in a discussion paper on Thursday.

items.title: India's IT giants grapple with weak growth despite resilient deal momentum
items.summary: Infosys reported results below expectations, impacting its stock performance negatively.  Other major IT companies also showed weak revenue growth in dollar terms.  AI-driven sales are gaining traction, though still on a small base.  Profitability varied across companies, with some margins contracting and others improving.  Stock valuations reflect uncertainty over future growth prospects and market conditions.

items.title: Saudi oil sales to India face 50% freight hike amid Houthi Red Sea blockade as ships eye longer Suez Canal route
items.summary: Saudi Arabia in India's third-largest oil supplier this month. The blockade primarily affects oil shipments &nbsp;from Yanbu port, which has emerged as one of the key alternatives for Saudi energy supplies for oil importers including India over the past few months.

items.title: Indian refiners scout new crude sources as Gulf risks rise
items.summary: Indian refiners are seeking alternative crude oil suppliers amid regional conflict.  They are exploring new sources from Venezuela and Angola to ensure supply.  Escalating attacks have disrupted vital shipping lanes and increased oil prices.  Refiners are considering rerouting shipments around Africa to avoid the Red Sea.  This shift will significantly lengthen journey times for crude oil deliveries.

items.title: India allows FDI in ecomm inventory model for exports
items.summary: The restrictions on foreign investment in inventory-based ecommerce will “not apply in case of exports of domestically manufactured and/or produced goods/products,” the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) said in a Press Note. The government has been considering this since last year in a broader effort to boost India’s exports without impacting the businesses of small retailers, people aware of the matter said.

items.title: CLO Price Swings Dictated by Tokyo, Wharton-Led Study Shows
items.summary: The $1.3 trillion US CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street.

items.title: GIC Posts Worst Five-Year Return Since 2013; US Still Top Market
items.summary: Singaporean sovereign wealth fund GIC Pte reported its lowest five-year annualized return in more than a decade, after the giant investor reduced some of its risks earlier on and its bond holdings didn’t fully rebound.

items.title: U.S. unveils new tariffs on 60 partners as Trump rebuilds trade agenda
items.summary: The levies, which take effect July 24, range from 10% to 12.5% and impact major economies like China, India and the European Union

items.title: Heat Worries US Wheat Farmers Just as Crop Prices Surge
items.summary: For farmers in North Dakota surveying their wheat as harvest closes in, scorching heat is threatening yields just as crop prices rise to a three-year high.

items.title: Dollar Rises as Intensifying Iran War Lifts Rate Hike Bets
items.summary: The dollar jumped Thursday as intensifying conflict in the Middle East sparked concerns over energy supply disruptions, fueling expectations that interest rates will stay higher for longer.

items.title: ​Core upgrade: On the Index of Core Industries
items.summary: The updated Index of Core Industries rounds out economic data upgrades

items.title: South African Deputy Minister Quits as $220 Billion Fund’s Chair
items.summary: South Africa’s deputy finance minister quit as the chairman of a state-owned firm that oversees $220 billion of government pensions, days before he faced being removed from the post.

items.title: US stocks today: Nasdaq plunges 2% as tech earnings spark AI spending worries
items.summary: Wall Street closed sharply lower with Nasdaq down over 2% as weak Big Tech earnings and rising AI spending concerns hit sentiment. Surging oil prices above $100, driven by Middle East tensions, reignited inflation fears and pushed bond yields higher, overshadowing gains in defense stocks like Lockheed Martin.

items.title: Yen’s Slump Extends Beyond Dollar and Stokes Inflation Fears
items.summary: While the yen’s plunge to a four-decade low against the dollar has dominated headlines, a broader gauge of the Japanese currency’s health is also flashing a warning.

items.title: Tesla short sellers mint $4 billion profit as shares nosedive
items.summary: Tesla shares plunged up to 15% after weak quarterly earnings, triggering about $4.1 billion in one-day gains for short sellers. Concerns over AI spending, high valuation, and soft auto margins weighed on sentiment, though retail investors continued buying and some analysts see the decline as a long-term opportunity.

items.title: Regulator warns insurers against deep discounts on fire risk cover
items.summary: The insurance regulator has flagged aggressive underpricing in fire insurance, with discounts of up to 99% on large industrial risks, warning it could hurt insurers’ financial health and underwriting discipline. Fire insurance premiums fell sharply in Q1 FY27 to ₹8,087 crore from ₹11,206 crore a year ago, after strong growth in FY26.

items.title: Regulator warns insurers against deep discounts on fire risk cover
items.summary: The insurance regulator has flagged aggressive underpricing in fire insurance, with discounts of up to 99% on large industrial risks, warning it could hurt insurers’ financial health and underwriting discipline. Fire insurance premiums fell sharply in Q1 FY27 to ₹8,087 crore from ₹11,206 crore a year ago, after strong growth in FY26.

items.title: Education Ministry reshuffle: Naresh Pal Gangwar named Higher Education Secretary, Vineet Joshi moved
items.summary: The reshuffle also appoints Sushil Kumar Lohani to head the Animal Husbandry Department and T K Anil Kumar as School Education Secretary.

items.title: Global demand puts India's two-wheeler exports back in top gear
items.summary: Bajaj Auto and TVS Motor reported strong international sales in the June quarter.  This marks a significant turnaround for India's two-wheeler export market.  Total motorcycle and scooter exports rose nearly thirty-seven percent year-on-year.  Demand across Africa, Latin America, and Asia is powering this export growth.  The domestic market also shows resilience, aided by recent GST reductions.

items.title: Clean power capacity all set to cross 300 GW: Pralhad Joshi
items.summary: India is set to cross 300 GW of non-fossil fuel power capacity soon, with current capacity at 297 GW, accounting for over half of total installed power.  The government targets 500 GW non-fossil capacity by 2030 as part of its clean energy push. The PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana is expected to cover over 5 million households in the coming weeks, boosting rooftop solar adoption.

items.title: B9 board, investors set out to find buyers for founder Ankur Jain's stake
items.summary: Bira 91 is currently exploring options for selling founder Ankur Jain's stake, with its board and investors actively pursuing potential buyers. They have reached out to various family offices and corporate entities to facilitate the sale. To resume operations, the company estimates an infusion of approximately ₹500 crore will be necessary.

items.title: ASG shareholder approaches NCLT against co-founder, alleges oppression
items.summary: In her petition at the Mumbai NCLT under Sections 241 and 242 of the Companies Act, Gang sought to restrain the company from amending its Articles of Association (AoA) to facilitate a proposed ₹450-crore financing deal by Singhvi for investing in ASG Hospitals, contending that the move violates contractual protections granted to her as a minority shareholder.

items.title: Primus, HDFC Capital to set up Rs 2,000 crore senior living platform
items.summary: Primus Senior Living and HDFC Capital have launched a Rs 2,000 crore investment platform to develop senior living communities across six major Indian cities, marking what they describe as the country's largest institutional investment in the segment. The partnership aims to expand purpose-built, institutional-grade senior housing through ownership and rental models, targeting a rapidly growing elderly population while addressing the sector's limited supply of professionally managed communities.

items.title: Cross-ownership could create conflict of interest, says IndiGo MD; airline posts Rs 382 crore Q1 loss despite 20% revenue growth
items.summary: IndiGo warns against airport operators owning airlines, citing a massive conflict of interest.  The airline reported a significant quarterly loss due to soaring jet fuel prices.  Government officials are considering a proposal to relax ownership rules for airport operators.  IndiGo's revenue increased, but expenses rose sharply, impacting profitability.  Capacity growth is expected to remain flat for the current quarter.

items.title: Jackson Labs seeks restoration of its licence
items.summary: The cancellation issued by the Punjab government followed reports that five women in Kota, Rajasthan, died after allegedly being administered an Oxytocin injection that upon testing found contained no active ingredient.

items.title: Pulses import bill shrinks as global prices fall, local production rises
items.summary: India's import bill for key pulses significantly decreased in FY26 due to robust domestic production. Lower global prices and import curbs also contributed to this substantial reduction. Exports of certain pulses, like Kabuli chana, saw a notable increase during the same period. This shift reflects India's growing self-sufficiency and improved market position for these commodities. Domestic policies and strong harvests have bolstered India's pulse sector performance.

items.title: Dollar hits new 40-year high versus yen, euro softer after ECB stands pat on rates
items.summary: GLOBAL-FOREX/ (UPDATE 6, GRAPHICS):FOREX-Dollar hits new 40-year high versus yen, euro softer after ECB stands pat on rates

items.title: Parliamentary panel backs Sebi board expansion, longer cooling-off period for top brass
items.summary: Standing Committee on Finance, headed by Bhartruhari Mahtab, backs key provisions of the Securities Markets Code Bill, including raising Sebi board strength to 15 from 9, and calls for a regulatory framework for virtual digital assets

items.title: For Nestle, India a growth outlier across markets
items.summary: Nestle SA has reported impressive growth in India, a significant market for the company. The packaged food giant achieved double-digit real internal growth in this region. While global sales experienced a slight decline in the first half of 2026, Nestle increased its full-year organic sales growth forecast. Notably, India's unit profit saw substantial growth, driven by increased volumes and strategic investments.

items.title: India's oil routes under pressure as second West Asia chokepoint shuts
items.summary: India faces intensified energy supply challenges due to choke point closures.  Three Saudi oil tankers rerouted in the Red Sea amid Houthi blockade.  Non-Saudi tankers bound for India via Bab al-Mandeb have not faced disruptions.  India sources oil from alternative locations like Fujairah terminal.  Long-term blockades in Hormuz and Bab al-Mandeb could significantly hurt India.

items.title: SC nudges Centre to amend Highways Act, wants judicial officers deciding land compensation disputes, not bureaucrats
items.summary: The Supreme Court urged the Centre to amend the National Highways Act.  It wants judicially trained adjudicators for land compensation disputes.  Currently, government officers decide these compensation matters.  The Attorney General stated the government is considering proposed amendments.  The court will await the government's decision on this issue.

items.title: Gold falls 2% as Middle East tensions fuel inflation fears, rate-hike bets
items.summary: GLOBAL-PRECIOUS:Gold falls 2% as Middle East tensions fuel inflation fears, rate-hike bets

items.title: Primus, HDFC Capital to invest  ₹2,000 cr in rental housing platform for seniors
items.summary: Primus Senior Living and HDFC Capital will jointly build a  ₹2,000 crore rental housing platform for seniors across six cities. The deal marks Primus's shift toward a rental-led model as India's under-penetrated senior living sector draws fresh institutional capital.

items.title: Blackstone profits jump, boosted by exits and bets on AI
items.summary: Blackstone Inc. reported a 26% jump in distributable earnings for the second quarter, buoyed by profits from exits and its artificial intelligence-related investments.

items.title: Houthi attack on Saudi tankers drives Brent past $100 per barrel, stresses India’s crude routes
items.summary: The price for a barrel climbed 6.2% to $99.94 and briefly topped the century mark, the first time the most actively traded contract has done so in two months

items.title: WTO nations query India’s PLI, QCO, import substitution, New Delhi says public policy objectives key
items.summary: WTO members questioned India's trade policies at a recent review. Concerns included quality orders and abrupt tariff changes. India defended its policies as vital for public objectives. Schemes like PLI and agricultural support also faced scrutiny. Members sought greater transparency and predictability in trade practices.

items.title: Infosys Q1 FY27 Results: Net profit slips 8.6% QoQ to Rs 7,769 crore; revenue rises 3.9%, margins inch up
items.summary: Revenue from operations rose 3.9 per cent quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) to Rs 48,211 crore, compared with Rs 46,402 crore in the March quarter.

items.title: Nandini butter and ghee prices hiked
items.summary: Bengaluru Cooperative Milk Union has increased prices for Nandini ghee and butter. New rates for various pack sizes will take effect from Friday. Retailers suggest a modest hike for ghee and a steeper rise for butter. A one-litre ghee sachet will now cost Rs 715. Butter prices also saw adjustments across different pack sizes.

items.title: Air India, Saudia sign MoU to expand codeshare, strengthen India-Saudi air connectivity
items.summary: The agreement aims to boost connectivity, explore terminal co-location, integrate loyalty programmes and identify new commercial opportunities.

items.title: Government bars communication infra providers from sharing data outside India
items.summary: New government rules mandate all telecom data must remain stored within India.  Communication infrastructure providers cannot share any data outside the country.  This new framework aims to enhance national digital infrastructure security.  It also introduces a new category for cloud-hosted telecommunication network providers.  The government can inspect sites and audit systems for compliance purposes.

items.title: Cipla Q1 net dips 39% to ₹785.55 crore as North America business drags
items.summary: We expect continued sequential growth in North America, supported by upcoming product pipeline, MD and Global CEO Achin Gupta said

items.title: Consumers complain about 17.22 lakh pesky calls, SMS till May this year: MoS Telecom
items.summary: Telecom subscribers reported over 17 lakh pesky communications this year.  Over 31 lakh such communications were reported in the year 2025.  More than 21 lakh unregistered telemarketer connections were disconnected since January 2024.  Around 24.53 crore consumers registered preferences on the Do Not Disturb platform.  AI solutions flagged over 26.4 crore suspected spam calls and messages daily.

items.title: Sebi mulls overhaul of online dispute resolution framework; seeks public comments till August 13
items.summary: The MIIs have better control and enforceability over the intermediaries and listed entities, and most of them are registered with MIIs in some capacity, said SEBI

items.title: Maruti Suzuki’s New Brezza receives 5-star Bharat NCAP safety rating
items.summary: Maruti’s  5-star Bharat NCAP-rated vehicle portfolio includes Dzire, VICTORIS, Invicto and e VITARA.

items.title: Infosys clocks weakest Q1 in six years, picks Dash for CEO
items.summary: Bengaluru-based Infosys marked a slow start to the fiscal year, even though it met analysts' expectations and outperformed three of its four large rivals. The company clocked $5.08 billion in revenue in the June quarter, up 0.83% sequentially and 2.8% on a yearly basis.

items.title: Cipla expects sequential recovery in North America on new product launches despite tariff uncertainty
items.summary: Cipla reported a 39% drop in net profit to ₹789 crore for the June quarter due to weakness in its North America business and margin compression, despite posting record revenue of ₹7,119 crore. EBITDA declined 33%, with margins narrowing significantly. Growth was driven by strong performance in India, Africa, and emerging markets, with the domestic business hitting an all-time high. The company remains focused on new product launches, including respiratory and peptide segments, and expects a recovery in the US business led by Ventolin supplies.

items.title: Flooding in South Gujarat disrupts rail traffic to Mumbai; 14 trains, including Vande Bharat and Shatabdi, cancelled
items.summary: Flooding in South Gujarat forced Western Railway to suspend operations on a key rail bridge between Gholvad and Umargam Road, disrupting train services between Gujarat and Mumbai

items.title: Cipla in no hurry to launch semaglutide as Yurpeak gains traction
items.summary: Cipla is focusing on its tirzepatide brand Yurpeak in India's obesity market. Yurpeak has become the second largest GLP-1 product since its launch. The company may not launch generic semaglutide if Yurpeak demand continues. Cipla will re-evaluate semaglutide opportunities in international markets later. Yurpeak's doctor acceptance and distribution give Cipla a competitive edge.

items.title: Government eases FDI rules for e-commerce companies aimed at export, first big relaxation in years
items.summary: While some trade experts say the move has provided much-needed clarity, others have said it will benefit foreign firms such as Amazon and will eventually hurt India’s small traders

items.title: Regular payments from Bangladesh help in reducing its dues to Adani Power
items.summary: Adani Power seeks to reduce ₹3,863 crore receivables from Bangladesh as monthly payments stabilise, enhancing liquidity.

items.title: Ramco Systems Q1 revenue down 3% at $18.6 million
items.summary: Annual recurring revenue stood at $12.15 million while the  unexecuted order book was at $152.3 million, the company said in an exchange filing

items.title: Centre considering lifting restrictions on airport ownership
items.summary: The current rules cap airline ownership in airports and are inferred to similarly restrict airport operators from owning airlines

items.title: Primary market shows signs of revival even as uncertainty looms
items.summary: SEBI, in April, extended the one year validity of IPO observation letters expiring between April 1 and September 30 to uniformly until September-end

items.title: FASTag Rules Explained: Can you use one FASTag for multiple vehicles? Check inside
items.summary: One FASTag can be used for one vehicle only because the VRN (Vehicle Registration Number is linked to one FASTag only. This FASTag is mapped to that vehicle in the National Electronic Toll Collection (NETC) system. Therefore, each vehicle must have its own separate FASTag. Even a family with different cars can't use the same FASTag for different vehicles.

items.title: Bank’s credit-deposit ratio touches a 62-year high of 82.6% in Q1FY27
items.summary: Loans grew faster than deposits. Variance between loan and deposit growth widest at 5 percentage points

items.title: Hindalco evaluating Rs 50,000 crore investments, says chairman Kumar Mangalam Birla
items.summary: Hindalco Industries is evaluating investments up to fifty thousand crore rupees. This potential investment pipeline will support the company's ambitious global expansion programs. Aluminium consumption in India is expected to increase five-fold by twenty forty-seven. Copper consumption is also projected to rise four-fold over the same period. The company aims to meet India's growing demands for metal solutions.

items.title: India plans Phase-2 of strategic petroleum reserves under PPP model at estimated ₹14,527 crore
items.summary: Phase-I of the SPR program added cumulative capacity of 5.33 MMT of crude in Vishakhapatnam (1.33 MMT), Mangaluru (1.5 MMT) and Padur (2.5 MMT).

items.title: Eight Roads, Flipkart among sellers in ₹1,048 crore Shadowfax block trade
items.summary: The deal involves up to 9.08% of the company’s total outstanding shares, with a floor price of ₹197 per share, representing a 9.9% discount to the July 23 closing price of ₹218.58

items.title: Tesla falls 15%, Alphabet drops 8% as AI spending concerns mount
items.summary: Tesla shares fell to around $319 following disappointing quarterly earnings, marking their lowest since August 2025. Alphabet also declined despite higher revenue, with concerns about rising AI investment overshadowing positive results. Both companies contributed to a 1.55% drop in the S&amp;P 500.

items.title: Copper market will likely witness volatility on West Asia developments
items.summary: Analysts say the red metal is currently caught between macro headwinds and US tariff uncertainty

items.title: Infosys posts  12% rise in Q1 net profit; names Ashiss Kumar Dash CEO-designate
items.summary: Salil Parekh said that he enjoyed leading Infosys from $10 billion in revenue to over $20 billion in revenue through during his tenure of two terms spanning nine years which would conclude on March 31, 2027

items.title: Elevate Campuses plans to raise ₹2,550 cr via IPO in August
items.summary: Elevate Campuses to use ₹1,100 crore of IPO proceeds to acquire portfolio of 14 K-12 school assets and campuses from affiliates of its promoters

items.title: Indian egg prices crack new record as heat, feed costs bite
items.summary: Supply tightness keeps egg prices elevated; chicken prices firm up too

items.title: ITC’s agri business becomes India’s largest private-sector agri enterprise, doubles revenue in five years
items.summary: ITCMAARS has onboarded over 2.6 million farmers across 11 States and aims to reach 10 million by 2030 through digital advisory, precision agriculture and climate-smart farming

items.title: Suryoday SFB Q1 profit more than doubles on strong loan growth
items.summary: Suryoday Small Finance Bank reported a more than twofold rise in first-quarter net profit to Rs 75 crore, driven by healthy business growth. Net interest income, operating profit and advances increased strongly, while gross NPAs improved significantly from a year earlier.

items.title: ITC “rearchitecting” cigarette portfolio to tackle challenges arising from sharp increase in taxes
items.summary: During the AGM, several shareholders raised concerns about a fall in ITC’s share prices after the government substantially increased taxes on cigarettes to around 70 per cent

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Friday
items.summary: Sensex and Nifty declined for a fourth straight session as surging crude oil prices and escalating US-Iran tensions dampened investor sentiment. Analysts flagged rising inflation risks, bearish technical indicators and continued market volatility, with Nifty likely to face resistance near the 24,000 mark.

items.title: SEBI weighs risk-based margin framework to boost cash market participation
items.summary: The proposal could lower upfront margins for trades in highly liquid stocks by around 10-15 percent

items.title: ₹70,300-crore auto-component investment wave gathers pace through FY29 on EV, localisation push
items.summary: The scale of the pipeline points to a shift from incremental capacity additions towards technology-led expansion across the component industry

items.title: No ethanol in premium petrol; no plans to restore E0/E10 petrol: Government
items.summary: Premium petrol grades will continue without ethanol blending, the government stated.  No decision has been made to increase ethanol blending beyond twenty percent.  The government will not restore E0 or E10 petrol supply nationwide.  Current E20 fuel has shown no widespread adverse engine impacts.
